label: AK(3) z=w word-choice sweep
note: Stabilize AK(3) to 3 generators with a chosen relator z=w(x,y), then run the greedy substitution solver. w is a free choice, so ~100 literature-grounded words are tried at up to 1,000,000 nodes on both AK(3) forms. Reaching the trivial 3-generator presentation would trivialize AK(3). A solved word writes a replayable path.

textbook.desc: textbook AK(3)  ⟨x,y | xyx=yxy, x³=y⁴⟩ — where the change-of-variables words are provably isolatable

rep.desc: dataset representative 13_1 (ms_reps_unsolved idx 0) — the exact object in the data

relhalf.desc: relator sides xyx, yxy, x³, y⁴ + rotations/inverses (Fagan z=xyx & alts)

wk.desc: y⁻ᵏ·x⁻¹yxy, k∈[-8,8] (paper Thm 6/7: exact valid isolations of x)

comm.desc: commutators + short double commutators (Appendix F bridge)

brute.desc: all freely-reduced words of length ≤3 (breadth probes)

control.desc: the dumb baselines w = r1, w = r2 (form-dependent)
